sentences.text: Task 1. Listen to the text and for each question mark the correct answer.
A, B, C or D.
You now have 40 seconds to look through the task.
You will then hear the recording twice. Good morning ladies and gentlemen. Welcome to our
sightseeing tour. I'm your tour guide. My name is Jack Carter. We'll start our sightseeing tour
of the capital of Guernsey in a few minutes. Many people don't know that Guernsey is an island in
the English Channel situated off the coast of Normandy, northwest of France. As you know,
we are in the capital of Guernsey called St. Peter Port. You can see Castle Cornet on your
right, an ancient historic structure with nearly 800 years of history. Once surrounded by the sea,
Castle Cornet is now accessible on foot. Actually, a symphony orchestra is performing
Beethoven's Ninth Symphony in Castle Cornet this Saturday night. Concerts of classical music are
often held there. But if you're interested in jazz, it's better to go to St. James
Concerts.
Concert Hall. Now, just in front of you is the Candy Gardens with its diverse plant collection.
Dear visitors, look to your right. This is the famous Hortville House where the great French
writer Victor Hugo lived. Located around the harbour, you can find a number of charming
shops and cafes that are great for taking a pleasant coffee break. Additionally, if you are
someone who enjoys walking, I have a list of the various guided walks available. It is unfortunately
that some guided tours are closed at this time of the year. But you can buy our booklet of self-guided
walks and visit the places by yourself. Let's see what's on today. Well, there's only one guided
walk today and it's around the capital. The guided walk which lasts an hour and a half and
starts at three o'clock, costs five pounds. You'll meet your guide in front of the information centre
where you can also pay for the tour. The guided tour will take you around the map to the city.
There are many interesting places to enjoy in St. Peter Port such as the peaceful canal,
beaches, hills with a wide variety of birds. But tourists are especially fascinated by the
lovely pointed cliffs around the canal. As long as people live, these cliffs will always attract
the nature lovers. At this time of the year, we also have Tenorfest, which is our food festival.
Tenorfest was launched in 1998 and we had no idea then that it would become the
Channel Islands' biggest food festival. It's a great festival. It should be noted that only the
best restaurants are allowed to participate in the festival. And it's called Tenorfest because
everything costs a tenor, or in other words, ten pounds. Lots of restaurants offer really
good meals for just ten pounds. I have the list of the restaurants that participate in the festival.
So, those of you who are interested in it, come over and I'll give you the list.
Tenorfest lasts a full three hours and it's a great festival. It's a great festival. It's a great
festival. If you plan to stay here for another couple of days, you can enjoy not only Guernsey's
traditional cuisine, but also Chinese, Indian, Italian, and other traditional flavors as Tenorfest
starts on Saturday, which is in two days' time. The restaurants are all good, but last year I had
crab at the Atlantic Hotel. It was excellent. I'd recommend that you try that place. But mind,
the ten pound menus aren't available all day, so make sure you check the leaflets.
For example, the menu at the Atlantic Hotel that I've recommended is only available from 12 until
2 but it's really worth it. By the way, if you're interested in visiting Jersey Island,
you can take a ferry there as ferry trips are cheaper during Tennerfest.
